title: Payroll Deduction File Generation — Record Per Active Employee Enrollment
description: The system shall generate a payroll deduction file based on approved employee benefit elections. Each record represents one employee's deduction for one benefit type. The file is generated as CSV (demo) with each record including all fields required for the payroll system to apply the correct deduction.

title: Payroll Deduction Calculation — Per-Pay-Period Amount for All Four Frequencies
description: The system shall calculate employee payroll deductions from the monthly employee premium using the authoritative formula: Pay-Period Deduction = ROUND((Monthly Premium × 12) ÷ Pay Periods Per Year, 2). All four pay frequencies must be supported. Calculation is centralized in the Premium & Deduction Calculation Service.
formula: ROUND((Monthly Premium × 12) ÷ Pay Periods Per Year, 2)

title: Payroll File Pre-Transmission Validation
description: Before transmitting to the payroll system, the system shall validate all records in the payroll deduction file. Validation failures produce a pre-flight report showing record counts, warnings, and errors. The user can transmit valid records while investigating errors, or hold the entire batch.
validationRules: Payroll ID exists in identity mapping
Plan ID references an active published plan
Deduction code exists and is active for the current plan year
Deduction amount matches premium calculation output
Effective date is valid and not in the past
Employee is currently eligible for the plan
Enrollment record is in ACTIVE or TRANSMITTED status
No duplicate transaction for the same employee + plan + effective date

title: Payroll Transmission Lifecycle States
description: Each payroll transmission batch shall follow a defined lifecycle. State transitions are recorded in the audit trail. The failure path supports investigation and resubmission without creating a new batch.

stateTransitions.trigger: Payroll system rejects batch or records

title: Payroll Transmission — Manual, Scheduled, and Retry Modes
description: The system shall transmit validated payroll deduction files to the configured payroll endpoint. Three trigger modes are supported: manual (Integration Admin clicks [Transmit to Payroll]), scheduled (cron-based at configured frequency), and retry (resubmit rejected records after correction). For the demo, transmission uses a mock payroll API that returns deterministic or chaos-mode responses.
demoEndpoint: Mock Payroll API — same deterministic/chaos pattern as carrier simulation

title: Payroll Acknowledgment — Accept, Partial, Reject, Timeout
description: The system shall receive and process a payroll acknowledgment response after transmission. The acknowledgment contains a record-level result for each transmitted record. The batch result is calculated from the individual record outcomes.

title: Payroll Rejection Handling — Capture Rejection Code, Affected Employee, and Recommended Fix
description: For each rejected payroll record, the system shall capture the rejection code, a human-readable description, the affected employee, plan, and deduction code. The rejection detail view links to the AI Exception Copilot for root cause analysis and resolution recommendation.

demoRejection.rejectionDescription: Deduction code MED-EE-FAM is not configured for the 2027 payroll period.

title: AI Payroll Exception Resolution — Root Cause, Impact, and Corrective Action
description: The AI Operations Agent shall analyze payroll transmission failures and produce a structured exception report: root cause, affected employees count, financial impact estimate, recommended resolution, and a corrective action button. This is the payroll equivalent of the carrier exception copilot (REQ-AI-COP-009).

response.rootCause: 42 records reference deduction code MED-EE-FAM which is not active in the 2027 payroll period.
response.recommendation: Update deduction code mapping: MED-EE-FAM → MED-PPO500-FAM-2027

humanCheckpoint: Integration Admin approves the mapping correction before revalidation and retransmission

title: Payroll Transmission Monitor — Dedicated Dashboard Panel
description: The Integrations workspace shall include a dedicated Payroll Transmission Monitor panel alongside the Carrier Integration Monitor. The panel shows the last transmission summary, current status, and quick-access actions.

title: Payroll Transmission History — Filterable Ledger of All Transmission Batches
description: The system shall maintain a paginated history of all payroll transmission batches with summary statistics per batch. The history is filterable by date range, status, employee, plan, benefit type, and error code.

title: Payroll Retry & Resubmission — Correct, Revalidate, Retransmit
description: The system shall allow authorized Integration Admins to retry failed payroll records after correcting the root cause. Retry supports three scopes: all failed records, a selected subset, or a single employee. Idempotency keys prevent duplicate deduction submissions.
retryWorkflow: System applies fix (e.g. deduction code remapping)
idempotencyKey: SHA-256(employerId + employeeId + planId + deductionCode + effectiveDate + transmissionVersion)

title: Payroll Reconciliation — Three-Way Match: Benefits vs. Payroll vs. Carrier
description: The reconciliation process shall perform a three-way match between: (1) the benefit enrollment record, (2) the payroll deduction applied, and (3) the carrier enrollment confirmation. A reconciled employee has matching data across all three systems. Mismatches are classified by type and surfaced in the Reconciliation workspace.

reconciliationOutcomes.RECONCILED: Benefits enrollment + payroll deduction + carrier enrollment all match
reconciliationOutcomes.PREMIUM_MISMATCH: Expected deduction ≠ actual payroll deduction applied
reconciliationOutcomes.MISSING_PAYROLL_RECORD: Benefit enrollment exists; no payroll deduction found
reconciliationOutcomes.MISSING_CARRIER_RECORD: Benefit enrollment exists; no carrier confirmation

demoScenarios.note: Old EE+Spouse rate still running after divorce

title: Payroll Transmission Schedule — Configurable Frequency with Manual Override
description: The system shall support configurable payroll transmission frequency: daily, weekly, biweekly, monthly, or manual-only. For the demo, the default is manual trigger plus a scheduled simulation option. The next scheduled transmission date and time are displayed on the Payroll Transmission Monitor.
demoDisplay: Next Scheduled Transmission: Friday, Jan 29, 2027 — 2:00 AM

title: Payroll File Format — CSV for Demo with Mock API Acknowledgment
description: The demo payroll integration shall use CSV as the primary file format for the generated deduction file. The mock payroll API receives the CSV and returns a JSON acknowledgment. This is simpler and visually understandable for demo audiences.
demoStack: Mock Payroll API: POST /api/payroll/transmit (receives CSV as multipart)
Acknowledgment: JSON response { batchId, recordsReceived, recordsAccepted, recordsRejected, status }

title: End-to-End Payroll Integration Architecture — Parallel to Carrier Integration
description: The payroll integration shall mirror the carrier integration architecture: enrollment → file generation → validation → transmission → acknowledgment → reconciliation → AI operations agent. Both integrations feed into the same Reconciliation workspace and share the AI Exception Resolution copilot pattern.

architectureDiagram.leftBranch: Carrier Integration: Enrollment File → Carrier Mock API → Acknowledgment
architectureDiagram.rightBranch: Payroll Integration: Deduction File → Payroll Mock API → Acknowledgment
architectureDiagram.convergence: Reconciliation → AI Operations Agent → Detect / Explain / Resolve

demoJourney: Payroll file transmitted to mock API
Payroll rejects Linda's record (invalid deduction code PAY-0042)
AI Operations Agent investigates → identifies invalid deduction code
Benefits Admin approves deduction code mapping correction
System regenerates payroll record with corrected code
Linda sees updated deduction in Employee Portal My Deductions
